attributes.title: GamePulse - Regression Lab
attributes.description: Curated 90-day regression dashboard for driver, Proton, kernel, hardware, and low-level causality checks.

embeddableConfig.title: Game
GPU Driver
Proton Ver
OS Kernel

query.esql: FROM metrics-gamepulse.session-default | WHERE gamepulse.hardware.gpu.driver_version IS NOT NULL | STATS `Drivers` = COUNT_DISTINCT(gamepulse.hardware.gpu.driver_version)
FROM metrics-gamepulse.session-default | WHERE gamepulse.hardware.gpu.driver_version IS NOT NULL | STATS `Drivers` = COUNT_DISTINCT(gamepulse.hardware.gpu.driver_version)

embeddableConfig.title: GPU Driver Versions Tested

query.esql: FROM metrics-gamepulse.session-default | WHERE gamepulse.compatibility.proton_version IS NOT NULL | STATS `Proton Versions` = COUNT_DISTINCT(gamepulse.compatibility.proton_version)
FROM metrics-gamepulse.session-default | WHERE gamepulse.compatibility.proton_version IS NOT NULL | STATS `Proton Versions` = COUNT_DISTINCT(gamepulse.compatibility.proton_version)

embeddableConfig.title: Proton Versions Tested

query.esql: FROM metrics-gamepulse.session-default | WHERE host.os.kernel IS NOT NULL | STATS `Kernels` = COUNT_DISTINCT(host.os.kernel)
FROM metrics-gamepulse.session-default | WHERE host.os.kernel IS NOT NULL | STATS `Kernels` = COUNT_DISTINCT(host.os.kernel)

embeddableConfig.title: Kernels Tested

query.esql: FROM metrics-gamepulse.session-default | WHERE gamepulse.summary.avg_fps IS NOT NULL | STATS `Peak FPS` = ROUND(MAX(gamepulse.summary.avg_fps), 0)
FROM metrics-gamepulse.session-default | WHERE gamepulse.summary.avg_fps IS NOT NULL | STATS `Peak FPS` = ROUND(MAX(gamepulse.summary.avg_fps), 0)

embeddableConfig.title: Peak Avg FPS Seen

query.esql: FROM metrics-gamepulse.session-default | WHERE gamepulse.summary.ended == true | STATS `Sessions` = COUNT(*)
FROM metrics-gamepulse.session-default | WHERE gamepulse.summary.ended == true | STATS `Sessions` = COUNT(*)

embeddableConfig.title: Total Sessions

query.esql: FROM metrics-gamepulse.audio-default | WHERE gamepulse.audio.backend IS NOT NULL | STATS `Audio Backends` = COUNT_DISTINCT(gamepulse.audio.backend)
FROM metrics-gamepulse.audio-default | WHERE gamepulse.audio.backend IS NOT NULL | STATS `Audio Backends` = COUNT_DISTINCT(gamepulse.audio.backend)

embeddableConfig.title: Audio Backends

FPS Over Time Coloured by GPU Driver — Each session coloured by the driver version running at the time. Driver updates that improve or regress FPS are visible as colour-shifted bands. Use the Game filter above to focus on a single title.
Avg FPS by GPU Driver Version — Which driver delivered the best performance? Compare avg FPS and 1% lows across driver versions.
Avg FPS by Proton Version — Which Proton/Wine version delivered the best compatibility and performance? Shows only sessions where Proton was active.
Avg FPS by Kernel Version — Kernel scheduler and GPU driver stack differences can affect framerate. Useful for identifying kernel regressions on gaming-tuned builds (CachyOS, Nobara, etc.).
Compatibility Matrix — Game × Proton Version × GPU Driver. Each row = one combination tested. Useful for validating compatibility before recommending a software stack for a specific title.
GPU Temperature Over Time — Most AMD/NVIDIA GPUs throttle above 100°C junction / 110°C hotspot. Watch for gradual temperature rise indicating throttling across a long session.
GPU Power Draw Over Time — Sustained high draw near card TDP = power-limited; watch for throttling. Power spikes on Vulkan/DX12 async compute reflect burst workloads.
CPU Utilisation Over Time — Total CPU utilisation and per-core load. High total% with low GPU util = CPU bottleneck. Uneven per-core load suggests the game is not using all available cores.
System Memory Usage Over Time — Game RSS = resident set size (physical memory used by the game process). High page cache hit rate is desirable; major page faults indicate RAM pressure.

GPU Pipeline Latency Over Time — GPU fence latency = time from command submission to GPU completion (measured by DRM fence callbacks). GPU scheduler latency = time GPU work spends queued waiting for execution. Spikes indicate GPU ring buffer saturation or driver overhead.

Stutter Severity Score Over Time — Composite score from frame drops + scheduler misses + I/O spikes. Score > 0.3 is noticeable; > 0.6 is severe. Correlate spikes with GPU pipeline and runqueue panels above.

CPU Scheduler Latency Over Time — Runqueue latency is the time game threads spend waiting for a CPU core. High values (>200 µs) indicate CPU saturation, conflicting affinity, or SMT interference. CCX migrations = cross-chiplet scheduler migrations on AMD Zen (expensive cache miss).
Block I/O Latency Over Time — High I/O latency during gameplay indicates shader cache compilation I/O or asset streaming bottleneck. Correlate with FPS drops on the Game Performance dashboard.
